response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but the role requires deep hardware-domain expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. His low-latency DLang systems work and brief C/C++ tooling experience at National Instruments are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of the principal-level silicon performance architecture background this position demands. There is no evidence of deep-learning workload optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, or performance modeling of hardware.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years educational path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack, not performance architecture — a principal-level domain gap

key_factors.factor: General low-latency systems and long tenure
